blockingIssues.description: localToUTC() in dashboard-client.tsx double-applies timezone offset. new Date('YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss') (no Z suffix) parses as browser-local time, which already equals the target TZ. The function then computes UTC-vs-TZ offset and adds it, effectively doubling the shift. For PDT (UTC-7): midnight PDT is correctly parsed as 07:00Z by the Date constructor, but then +7h is added giving 14:00Z — wrong by 7 hours. This causes incorrect todayStart/todayEnd, breaking today's task filtering and Done Today counts for any user not at UTC+0. The same bug pattern exists in dayBoundsUTC() in todo-board.tsx from the web-todos-page feature. Both must be fixed. Fix: use `return new Date('${dateStr}T${timeStr}').toISOString()` since browser TZ always equals target TZ, or use Z suffix and then apply offset correctly.

dayBoundsUTC() in todo-board.tsx has the same double-application timezone offset bug as localToUTC() in dashboard-client.tsx. Tasks due between midnight and ~7AM local time (for US Pacific users) would be excluded from Today/This Week filters. The All time group and status filtering are unaffected. Same fix needed.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Layout uses plain <a> tags instead of Next.js <Link> components, causing full page reloads on every navigation. Should use import Link from 'next/link'.
No unit tests for isOverdueActive() helper. VAL-STATUS-004 requires test output as evidence. Only a visual demo page exists.
Non-null assertions (!) on env vars in web/lib/supabase.ts without runtime validation.
getSupabaseClient() creates a new client on every fetchDashboardData() call. Other components cache the client.
getUserTimezone() duplicated in 4+ files (dashboard-client.tsx, todo-board.tsx, todo-card.tsx, demo page). Should be extracted to shared utility.
Overdue filter includes ALL in_progress tasks with past due_at, not just within 15-min grace window. Previous-day overdue tasks appear under Today.
Duplicated Supabase client singleton pattern (getSupabaseClient with cachedClient) between todo-board.tsx and todo-actions.ts.
fetchTodos defined inside component but not in useEffect dependency array — potential ESLint exhaustive-deps warning.
Duplicate HTML id attributes (edit-task-text, reschedule-datetime) across multiple TodoCards. Should use dynamic ids like edit-task-text-${todo.id}.
Missing Radix UI Dialog.Description in Edit, Reschedule, and Cancel dialogs. Creates console warning and reduces accessibility.
Reschedule hardcodes DEFAULT_LEAD_MINUTES=30 instead of fetching user's actual lead_minutes.
getSupabaseClient() creates new client on every 10s poll. Should cache like todo-actions.ts does.
Error state cleared at start of each poll, causing brief flash where error disappears then potentially reappears.
Some 'integration' tests are purely conceptual (local array filtering) rather than exercising actual code. Real verification done via browser testing.
Empty-state verification covered error states (Supabase connection failure) but not true zero-data states.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Use Next.js <Link> component (import Link from "next/link") for all internal navigation instead of plain <a> tags, to enable client-side transitions without full page reloads.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: web/app/layout.tsx uses 4 plain <a href> tags for sidebar navigation. Identified in web-status-badges review.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Shared browser-side utilities (getUserTimezone, Supabase client, timezone conversion) must be defined in web/lib/ and imported — never duplicated across component files.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: getUserTimezone() duplicated identically in 4+ files. getSupabaseClient() duplicated with inconsistent caching patterns in 4+ files. Identified across web-home-page, web-todos-page, web-manual-controls, and web-logs-page reviews.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'For client-side Supabase usage in web components, use the lazy-cached singleton pattern (module-level cachedClient with error handling) from web/lib/todo-actions.ts. Do not use the eager singleton from web/lib/supabase.ts (which crashes on missing env vars) or create uncached clients per call.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Four different Supabase client patterns coexist: eager singleton (supabase.ts), lazy-cached (todo-actions.ts, todo-board.tsx), and uncached (dashboard-client.tsx, logs/page.tsx). Identified across all feature reviews.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Create .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md skill for web dashboard workers. All 6 web-dashboard features referenced skillName 'frontend-worker' but the skill doesn't exist. Workers fell back to mission-worker-base only.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 6 feature reviews confirmed the frontend-worker skill is missing. Each worker's skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviation noting the skill was not found.

rejectedObservations.observation: Lock file commit policy should be documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: Already standard practice (package-lock.json committed for reproducible builds). Not worth adding explicit convention — it's obvious.

rejectedObservations.observation: Large initial commit (19 files, 3056 insertions) for web-status-badges violates atomic commits convention
rejectedObservations.reason: Justified exception — the web project didn't exist yet, so scaffolding had to be combined with the first feature. AGENTS.md already has sufficient commit discipline guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Supabase query chaining violates single-responsibility-lines convention
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in AGENTS.md. Multiple reviewers flagged it but it's consistently not followed in web code — this is a convention enforcement issue, not a missing convention. The short Supabase chains (.from().select().order()) are readable enough that strict enforcement may be counterproductive for web code.
